explicit_text: John Jacob Niles was a significant figure in my father's early musical life. Before high school, my father rode his bicycle out to Boot Hill because John Jacob Niles asked him to come teach music lessons for his children. This connection shows how deeply rooted my father's musical education was, linking him to notable musicians like Niles.

explicit_text: My mother was instrumental in starting the first Girl Scout troop in Eastern Kentucky along with Miss Thacker. She began with a Brownie troop when we were old enough and then moved on to Girl Scouts. Even though she was hard of hearing and didn’t sing with us, she was very supportive and loved dancing and community activities.
